analysis.overall_assessment: The 14-day data shows strong autonomic stability with excellent average HRV (60.9ms) and low resting HR (46 bpm), indicating good POTS management. However, concerning trends emerge in the final days with deep sleep dropping significantly (0.8h on 3/31 vs 1.4h on 3/29) and HRV crashing to 41ms on 3/31, suggesting autonomic strain that could impact all three conditions.
whats_going_well: Excellent autonomic baseline with average HRV of 60.9ms and stable resting HR averaging 46 bpm, indicating strong POTS control and parasympathetic dominance
Temperature deviations remain neutral at -0.0°C average with no concerning spikes, suggesting Hashimoto's is well-controlled without active immune flares
Strong readiness scores averaging 86/100 with peak of 92 on 3/26, indicating good nervous system capacity for vestibular rehabilitation exercises
areas_to_improve: Deep sleep consistency is problematic - dropped from 1.6h (3/28) to 0.6h (3/27) to 0.8h (3/31), averaging only 1.1h when 1.5-2h is optimal for immune regulation in Hashimoto's
HRV volatility shows concerning pattern with 41ms on 3/31 (33% below average), likely following the poor sleep cascade and indicating autonomic stress
Activity pacing may need adjustment - high step days (14,589 on 3/29, 13,304 on 3/27) preceded by or followed by lower HRV days, suggesting potential overexertion for POTS management
analysis.autonomic_assessment: Overall autonomic function appears well-managed with excellent average HRV (60.9ms) and bradycardic resting HR (46 bpm) indicating strong parasympathetic tone and POTS stability. However, the sharp HRV decline to 41ms on 3/31 following several days of poor deep sleep suggests the autonomic nervous system is becoming strained, requiring immediate attention to prevent POTS symptom recurrence.
analysis.thyroid_immune_signals: Temperature stability at 0.0°C deviation suggests no active Hashimoto's flares currently. However, declining deep sleep averaging only 1.1h (particularly 0.6-0.8h on recent days) is concerning as deep sleep is critical for immune system regulation and may predispose to future autoimmune activation.
analysis.vestibular_readiness: High readiness scores (88-92 on 3/28-3/29) indicate good capacity for vestibular rehab during optimal days. However, the readiness drop to 80-81 on 3/30-3/31 coinciding with low HRV suggests vestibular exercises should be scaled back or focused on gentler gaze stabilization rather than challenging balance work.
analysis.focus_for_next_week: Prioritize sleep architecture optimization to restore deep sleep above 1.2h nightly - this is the foundational metric driving the recent HRV decline and autonomic instability that threatens all three conditions.
